evals.prompt: Use $diagram-generator to turn evals/files/ecommerce-order-lifecycle.md into a Mermaid state diagram. Keep it readable, avoid edge-case clutter, and make any missing assumptions explicit.
evals.expected_output: A Mermaid stateDiagram-v2 wrapped in a clear markdown response, grounded in the provided lifecycle, with no Mermaid YAML frontmatter and no invented states beyond clearly marked assumptions.

evals.prompt: Use $diagram-generator to create a sequence diagram from evals/files/password-reset-flow.md. Focus on the core happy path and preserve the security-sensitive generic response behavior.
evals.expected_output: A Mermaid sequence diagram with the right participants, ordered interactions, and a concise title and purpose statement. The result should stay focused on the main flow instead of branching into every failure path.

evals.prompt: Use $diagram-generator to document the system described in evals/files/payments-platform-overview.md. Pick a compact 3-diagram bundle and generate it directly.
evals.expected_output: A compact bundle ordered from high-level to detailed, typically including a context/container view, one key behavioral diagram, and one structural or data view. Each diagram should have a short rationale and a standalone code block.

evals.prompt: Use $diagram-generator to convert evals/files/incident-auth-degradation.md into a Mermaid sequence diagram that highlights the grounded failure path, keeps assumptions explicit, and avoids turning speculation into fact.
evals.expected_output: A Mermaid sequence diagram focused on the failure timeline, with the main participants in the right order, a short title and purpose statement, and only clearly marked assumptions for any missing implementation detail.
